summary: Large lb_00F9 decompilation/matching PR centered on dynamics data, JObj traversal, GX/camera blur rendering, and header/type cleanup. The decomp.dev bot reported +3012 matched bytes, 7 new full matches in main/melee/lb/lb_00F9, and 11 additional unmatched-item improvements. The PR also renamed the polymorphic dynamics descriptor field from ft_unk to lb_unk0 and updated fighter/stage users accordingly, reflecting that the structure is library-owned rather than fighter-specific.

key_files.why: Main implementation work: replaced many nonmatching stubs/partial solutions with C for dynamics initialization/update, JObj traversal helpers, texture/GX setup, blur rendering, camera GObj creation, and utility callbacks.

Refined lb_00F9-related structs/unions: expanded lb_00F9_UnkDesc0, changed embedded fields to Quaternion/Vec3 where appropriate, removed ftDynamics_UnkDesc alias, added DynamicsData::unk_94, and typed static pool structs as entries arrays.
Updated public prototypes, including lb_800122F0 and the concrete signature for lb_800138EC.
Added static data declarations for lb_804D3768, lb_804D376C, and lb_804D63B8 used by the newly decompiled code.
Changed lbVector_CreateEulerMatrix parameter type from Vec3* to Quaternion*, matching how lb_00F9 stores rotations.
Header signature synchronized with lbvector.c for Quaternion-based Euler matrix creation.
Updated fighter dynamics call sites from desc.ft_unk.jobj to desc.lb_unk0.jobj.
Updated castle stage dynamics call site from desc.ft_unk.jobj to desc.lb_unk0.jobj.

what_changed: Implemented lb_8000FA94 to initialize/free-list the global DynamicsData pool of 0x140 entries and the lb_80011A50_t pool of 8 entries, setting cur_data, lb_804D63AC, lb_804D63B0, and lb_804D63B4.

Implemented lb_8000FD48 for building a DynamicsDesc chain from a child JObj sequence, caching rotate/scale/translate/world-position data, computing segment lengths, and deriving a dominant-axis indicator.

Added a large partial implementation of lb_8001044C, covering dynamic bone simulation pieces such as parent matrix setup, natural/current/link direction computation, stiffness, gravity, interaction forces, angular velocity, angle constraints, collider avoidance, and ground/floor checks.
Implemented lb_80011C18 as a recursive JObj/DObj rendermode flag propagator using checkJObjFlags and lb_80011B74.
Implemented lb_80011E24 as a variadic JObj lookup helper that walks a JObj tree by requested indices until -1.
Implemented lb_8001204C as an indexed JObj lookup helper taking a u16 indices array and count, reusing traversal state when indices are monotonic.
Implemented lb_800122F0 to configure GX texture/TEV state for drawing image textures, including optional multi-stage color-factor setup.
Improved lb_8001271C matching by restructuring the GX quad draw with explicit intermediate variables and a smaller PAD_STACK.
Implemented lb_80012994 to draw a blur-like multi-sample texture pass around a source image using repeated lb_8001271C calls with varying offsets and alpha values.
Implemented fn_80013614 for camera blur render behavior, setting current CObj, z mode, texture coordinate generation, channels, optional scissor, and drawing either blur or normal textured output.
Implemented lb_800138EC to allocate/setup an orthographic camera GObj and associated user data, using a default fn_80013614 render callback when none is supplied.
Implemented/matched additional lb_00F9 functions reported by the bot: lb_80014770, lb_80014258, lb_800138EC, lb_8001204C, lb_80011710, lb_8001271C, and lb_800100B0 reached 100% after the PR.
Renamed dynamics union access from desc.ft_unk to desc.lb_unk0 in ftdynamics.c and grcastle.c, and removed the ftDynamics_UnkDesc union member from types.h.
Changed lbVector_CreateEulerMatrix to accept Quaternion* instead of Vec3*, aligning the API with lb_00F9 rotation storage and call sites.
smart_moves: The ft_unk to lb_unk0 rename avoided leaving a library-owned polymorphic descriptor named after the fighter subsystem; this made fighter and stage users share the same neutral lb naming.
Typing lb_804D63A0_t and lb_804D63A8_t as real entries arrays instead of opaque padding made pool initialization code in lb_8000FA94 straightforward and searchable.
Splitting JObj index lookup into variadic lb_80011E24 and array-based lb_8001204C preserved two caller-facing APIs while sharing the same traversal model and monotonic-index optimization.
Using Quaternion and Vec3 fields in lb_00F9_UnkDesc0/Inner clarified offsets enough to support matrix/vector-heavy dynamics code without excessive casts.
The GX blur implementation was built from smaller helpers: lb_800122F0 configures texture/TEV state, lb_8001271C draws one quad, and lb_80012994 composes the repeated offset passes.
The decomp used explicit temporaries, casts, PAD_STACK annotations, and inline_depth pragmas where needed, indicating matching-driven source shaping rather than only semantic cleanup.
decomp_lessons: When an unknown union member is used by multiple systems, prefer a neutral library-scoped name over a subsystem-specific guess; desc.lb_unk0 was more appropriate than desc.ft_unk once grcastle also used it.
Pool-backed linked lists often become much easier to match after the backing static allocation is typed as an array of real entries rather than char padding.
For JObj traversal helpers, preserving traversal state across sorted or monotonic index requests can match original code structure and avoid restarting from root unnecessarily.
Quaternion-vs-Vec3 distinctions matter even when fields expose x/y/z names; lbVector_CreateEulerMatrix needed Quaternion* to line up with the rotation fields used by lb_00F9.
GX/TEV matching benefits from decomposing setup and draw passes into helpers that mirror original reuse: initialize texture state once, draw repeated quads through a shared primitive routine.
Large dynamics functions can be advanced substantially by first stabilizing struct offsets, saved JObj transforms, and small vector helpers before trying to finish every physics branch.
naming_conventions: Renamed union member ft_unk to lb_unk0 for library-owned dynamics data.
Kept unknown lb_00F9 fields in offset-style names such as unk_2C, unk_38, unk_44, unk_48, unk_54, unk_58, unk_68, and unk_8C when semantics were not proven.
Used lb_00F9_UnkDesc0 and lb_00F9_UnkDesc1/Inner for two alternative PolymorphicDesc layouts rather than overclaiming semantic names.
Named static globals by address-style project convention, e.g. lb_804D63A0, cur_data, lb_804D63AC, lb_804D63B0, lb_804D63B4, and lb_804D63B8.
Kept generated/address function names such as lb_8001044C, fn_80013614, and lb_800138EC because stronger semantic names were not evidenced in the PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations in sensitive functions such as lb_8000FD48, lb_8001044C, lb_800122F0/lb_80012994 paths, and fn_80013614 to control stack layout.
Used #pragma inline_depth around lb_80011C18, suggesting inline-depth tuning was needed around recursive JObj/DObj flag handling.
Introduced explicit signed casts and s32 temporaries for loop comparisons and fields, for example in dynamics count handling and inlineA1's max comparison.
Restructured lb_8001271C with deliberately placed temporaries and an odd-looking dead conditional to improve matching of GX quad emission.
Preserved floating-point literal forms and casts, including f32/f64 mixes in dynamics rotation calculations and constants like 0.015625f, 120.0f, 179.0f, and 26.099998f in GX color setup.
Replaced opaque padding with typed structs only where offsets/size assertions were maintained, including STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct DynamicsData) == 0x98) and pool sizes.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The only feedback was the decomp.dev bot report: matched code became 62.41%, +0.08%, +3012 bytes.
Bot reported 7 new full matches in main/melee/lb/lb_00F9: lb_80014770, lb_80014258, lb_800138EC, lb_8001204C, lb_80011710, lb_8001271C, and lb_800100B0.
Bot reported 11 additional improvements, notably lb_8001044C to 81.26%, lb_80012994 to 99.57%, lb_800122F0 to 91.72%, lb_8000FD48 to 93.66%, fn_80013614 to 99.07%, and lb_8000FA94 to 95.75%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish lb_8001044C after this 81.26% partial match.
Search for later renames of lb_00F9_UnkDesc0 fields such as unk_48, unk_54, unk_58, unk_68, and unk_8C once dynamics semantics are understood.
Check callers of lb_800138EC and fn_80013614 to determine whether lb_800138D8_t/CameraBlurData field names can be made semantic.
Review subsequent lbvector changes to see whether Quaternion* for lbVector_CreateEulerMatrix propagated cleanly to all call sites.
Look for later PRs replacing lb_unk0 with a semantic name for dynamics chain/bone data.
